steps.explanation: Start with $7m = 5(n + p)$. Divide both sides by $5$ to isolate the parenthesized expression: $$\frac{7m}{5} = n + p$$
Subtract $p$ from both sides to isolate $n$: $$n = \frac{7m}{5} - p$$
choices: • Choice A: $n = \frac{5p}{7m}$ — this inverts and conflates the terms; dividing $5p$ by $7m$ has no basis in the original equation. Eliminated.
• Choice B: $n = \frac{7m}{5} - p$ — dividing both sides by $5$ then subtracting $p$ gives exactly this expression. That matches choice B — B is correct.
• Choice C: $n = 5(7m) + p$ — this multiplies instead of divides and adds instead of subtracts. Eliminated.
• Choice D: $n = 7m - 5 - p$ — this subtracts $5$ as a separate term rather than dividing by it. Eliminated.
